zotero.abstractNote: In this paper, we propose a new distribution-free concept for representing, comparing, and propagating uncertainty in dynamical systems. Our approach is based on kernel probabilistic programming, which allows us to represent probability distributions as elements in a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS). This representation enables us to compare distributions using the maximum mean discrepancy (MMD) and to propagate uncertainty through dynamical systems using kernel-based operators. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmark problems, including a nonlinear dynamical system and a robotic arm control task.
